description: Defines every container type TradeFlow can target. Used by the source router to dispatch API calls, by the email writer to personalise outreach, and by the archetype matcher to resolve buyer_archetype_ids.

container_types.label: Care home
container_types.description: Residential facilities providing personal or nursing care to elderly or disabled adults. Regulated by the Care Quality Commission.
pain_points: CQC compliance pressure means maintenance cannot be deferred
High staff turnover leaves no in-house trades expertise
24/7 operation requires reliable out-of-hours response
Safeguarding obligations demand DBS-checked contractors

container_types.label: Hotel
container_types.description: Commercial accommodation businesses ranging from boutique independents to chain-managed properties.
pain_points: Guest-facing areas demand fast turnaround on any fault
Revenue-per-room pressure means planned maintenance is preferred over reactive callouts
Seasonal peaks (summer, Christmas) require pre-season works to complete on schedule
Branding standards require consistent finish quality across all rooms

container_types.label: School
container_types.description: State and independent schools including primary, secondary, and academies. Governed by local authority, multi-academy trust, or independent board.
pain_points: Works must be scheduled during holidays to avoid disrupting pupils
DfE and Ofsted compliance requires documented maintenance records
Budget approval cycles mean trades must quote early in the school year
Safeguarding policy requires DBS-checked, site-induction-aware contractors

container_types.label: Dental practice
container_types.description: NHS and private dental surgeries. Regulated by the Care Quality Commission. Strict infection-control and clinical environment standards.
cqc_service_types: Primary Medical Services and Integrated Care
pain_points: Clinical environment rules limit which trades can work in surgery areas
Downtime costs are very high — a closed surgery loses NHS UDA targets
Water, drainage, and compressed-air systems are business-critical
CQC inspections require documentary evidence of planned maintenance

container_types.label: Domiciliary care agency
container_types.description: Regulated providers of personal care delivered to people in their own homes. CQC-registered office premises, typically small.
pain_points: Office space is modest but must meet CQC accessibility requirements
Tight margins mean competitive pricing is essential
No in-house estates team — heavily reliant on trusted external trades

container_types.label: Pub / bar
container_types.description: Licensed on-trade venues including managed pubs, free houses, and cocktail bars.
pain_points: Beer cellar systems, gas lines, and extraction are business-critical
Licensing compliance and EHO visits create hard maintenance deadlines
Works outside trading hours required — early mornings or Mondays

container_types.label: Restaurant
container_types.description: Food-service businesses operating a kitchen and dining area. Independent and small-chain operators.
pain_points: Commercial kitchens have gas, extraction, and water systems all in constant use
EHO inspections create mandatory compliance deadlines
A closed restaurant during a fault means immediate lost revenue

container_types.label: Gym / leisure centre
container_types.description: Commercial fitness facilities including independent gyms, national chain branches, and council-run leisure centres.
pain_points: Pool and shower facilities require specialist plumbing and legionella control
Members expect facilities to be operational — closed changing rooms lose memberships
High moisture environments mean decoration and flooring need frequent attention

container_types.label: Office building
container_types.description: Multi-tenant or owner-occupied commercial office premises. Managed by a facilities or property manager.
pain_points: Multi-tenancy means works must not disrupt neighbouring businesses
M&E compliance (EICR, gas safety, fire systems) has hard annual deadlines
Facilities managers are managing multiple sites and need reliable supply chains

container_types.label: Nursery / early years setting
container_types.description: Ofsted-registered childcare and early education settings including day nurseries and pre-schools.
pain_points: Ofsted and EYFS framework require safe, well-maintained premises
Works must happen when children are not present
DBS-checked contractors are a non-negotiable requirement
Outdoor play areas and perimeter security are recurring maintenance needs
